posts.author: The Giraffe
posts.content:
Predictions: 10 of 14 Winners, 2 Perfect, 595 Points
|
Tied for 1580th

@madaMMA

A submission attempt is indeed scored as effective grappling.  But it proved to be a rather weak and insignificant attempt and he stayed on his back for 3 or so minutes not transitiong to a more advantageous position, and not locking up to a more substantive submission attempt.  He was just stuck doing nothing, holding on to an attempt that had already failed.  Taira demonstrated that he was perfectly fine, and those little rabbit punches he landed + transitioning to a front headlock at the end with those little knees was enough.  Taira in effect had his own effective grappling that scored, while also landing strikes.

It's not like it was a 4 minute sub attempt.  It was a sub attempt that failed in short order, and then he was stuck in that position, doing nothing, not transitioning, not locking in a stronger attempt.  That's the reason that Mark Smith (not everyone's favorite ref tonight) warned that he was going to break it up.  He was right to do that.

@Noxx - I picked Merab too because I thought he would do just his old game plan.... the issue was two things - one Merab had to cut weight 4-5 times for 4 fights in one year. That takes a toll on your body.... he might be a machine but age will get to every man eventually and cutting weight.
Two... Petr Yan came prepared because he already fought Merab and knew his game plan and what it felt like to be in the cage with him. So he trained hard to defend Takedowns in certain positions. Thats super effective... just think if JDM  trained specifically how Islam do takedowns and get sprawl or knock him off balance. There will not be any Domination by Islam machakchev.
